description_long: This street light features a tall, slender, dark-colored metal pole anchored to a square base, likely made of cast iron or steel, with a slightly flared section near the bottom for added stability. At the top, a classic lantern-style lamp head is mounted, characterized by a four-sided, tapered glass enclosure with a gold or brass-toned frame and a decorative finial at the peak. The lamp head is capped with a gently sloped, metallic roof, and the glass panels are frosted or semi-opaque to diffuse the light. Two short, horizontal arms extend from the pole just below the lamp head, possibly for decorative or structural purposes. The overall design is reminiscent of Victorian or early 20th-century urban street lighting, blending utilitarian function with ornamental details. The materials and construction suggest durability for outdoor urban environments.
description: A tall, dark metal street light with a classic lantern-style lamp head, gold-toned roof, and decorative finial, mounted on a square base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lantern head, gold roof, decorative finial, and both horizontal arms clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the lantern head and finial visible, but only one horizontal arm is seen; the pole appears slimmer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the lantern head, gold roof, and both arms, but with less detail on the lamp face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the lantern head and finial, with only one horizontal arm visible; the pole remains slender.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Classic lantern-style with a four-sided, tapered glass enclosure, gold or brass-toned frame, sloped metallic roof, and decorative finial.
